summary: A Tau-dispatched local wrapper invoked the pdf_oxide page harness, which then called SciLLM HTTP transport from pdf_oxide-owned code. This violates the human-stated boundary that Tau owns SciLLM/DAG agentic harness work.

boundary_violation.rule: pdf_oxide project agent must not make or wrap direct SciLLM calls for the second-pass model transport path; Tau must own that route.
boundary_violation.bad_effect: The wrapper called scripts/pdf_lab/run_page_second_pass_dag.py, which attempted POST /v1/scillm/opencode/transport/runs.
boundary_violation.corrective_action: Stop this path. Do not reuse or commit the wrapper as an accepted workflow. Treat the page29 live second-pass gate as blocked until Tau provides a native route or explicit work-order contract for the PDF Lab model transport.

local_receipts.tau_dag_proves: Tau parsed and dispatched one bounded local command node.
local_receipts.tau_dag_does_not_prove: It does not prove a Tau-native SciLLM adapter, provider/model quality, or a valid PDF Lab live second-pass review.
local_receipts.scillm_error_message: Client error '404 Not Found' for url 'http://localhost:4001/v1/scillm/opencode/transport/runs'

next_required_action.action: Provide a Tau-native work-order/DAG route for PDF Lab page second-pass model transport, or explicitly declare the accepted Tau-owned SciLLM surface and receipt contract.
next_required_action.pdf_oxide_action_until_then: Do not run live second-pass SciLLM transport from pdf_oxide. Continue only deterministic extraction/selection/artifact preparation that does not call SciLLM.

proves: A boundary violation occurred in the page29 attempt.
The page29 live second-pass model transport is not a valid progress artifact.
The immediate blocker is ownership/routing, not a semantic page29 extraction verdict.
does_not_prove: A working Tau-native PDF Lab model transport.
